title: Dizziness cardiac screening documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

source.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
source.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

assessment.text: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.
assessment.rationale: The NICE section supports documenting event history, posture, prodrome, witness context, previous events, medicines, family history, vital signs, postural blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological features when dizziness includes transient loss of consciousness or a blackout concern.

source.title: Transient loss of consciousness ('blackouts') in over 16s
source.population: Adults and young people aged 16 years and older with suspected transient loss of consciousness.
source.setting: Initial assessment, emergency transfer, referral and specialist evaluation of transient loss of consciousness.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected event-history, witness-account, vital-sign, cardiovascular or neurological assessment, ECG-result, urgent-risk and transfer-of-record documentation. It is international guidance and does not establish the diagnosis or UAE disposition.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1.1–1.1.2.1 — initial history, witness account, examination, and vital signs

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports event and witness history, posture, prodrome, appearance, movements, tongue biting, injury, duration, confusion, focal weakness, previous events, medical and family history, medicines, vital signs, lying and standing blood pressure, cardiovascular examination, and neurological signs.

assessment.text: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.
assessment.rationale: The NICE AF section supports pulse, ECG, and ambulatory-monitoring context when dizziness raises clinician suspicion of AF; it does not diagnose AF.

source.title: Atrial fibrillation: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected, diagnosed, or previously treated atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter.
source.setting: Primary, outpatient, acute, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-led AF detection, risk and cardiac-function assessment, documented care review, and anticoagulation review. Treatment recommendations, named medicines, risk-score conclusions, and doses are not mapped, and UAE pathways require local adaptation.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1-1.1.3 - detection, 12-lead ECG, and ambulatory ECG

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician assessment of relevant symptoms, pulse, ECG, and ambulatory ECG context without automatically diagnosing atrial fibrillation.

assessment.text: Supports urgent cardiovascular assessment for ECG abnormality, heart failure, transient loss of consciousness during exertion, family history of sudden cardiac death, new or unexplained breathlessness, or a heart murmur.
assessment.rationale: Cardiovascular red flags were reviewed as clinician-assessed prompts; escalation is never generated automatically.

source.exclusions: Exact for selected event-history, witness-account, vital-sign, cardiovascular or neurological assessment, ECG-result, urgent-risk and transfer-of-record documentation. It is international guidance and does not establish the diagnosis or UAE disposition.
